system_description.overview: Promptless is an AI teammate that automatically updates customer-facing documentation. The platform ingests trigger events (GitHub PRs, Slack DMs, Zendesk tickets) and uses AI to keep product docs current across multiple documentation platforms.

products.Promptless Web Application: Dashboard for managing AI-driven documentation automation
products.Promptless AI Engine: LLM-powered engine (via Anthropic) for generating and updating documentation
products.Integration Layer: Connects to GitHub/GitLab, Jira/Linear, Slack/Teams, Zendesk, and documentation platforms (ReadMe, Mintlify, Intercom, Docusaurus, GitHub Pages)

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — AWS controls excluded from scope)
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no owned/leased office; operating from co-working or remote; environmental controls managed by building management / AWS
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (daily backups with multi-AZ configured per CC7.4.2)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: VPC-based architecture. Network diagram shows API Gateway → Promptless EKS Cluster → S3 Document Store + RDS Database (Aurora). Segmented access via ACLs.
network_architecture.api_gateway: AWS API Gateway (traffic entry point per network diagram)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS/IPS via AWS — intrusion detection system monitors for unauthorized access and anomalies (CC2.1.1 confirmed in test results)
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS via established third-party certificate authority for all web-based system requests
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet via content filtering
network_architecture.topology: External triggers (GitHub PRs, Slack DMs, Zendesk tickets) and dashboard users hit AWS API Gateway, which routes to Promptless EKS Cluster. EKS pods write to S3 Document Store and RDS Aurora database. Anthropic provides LLM inference as external subprocessor. Datadog provides logging and monitoring. Clerk handles web-app user authentication.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test environment confirmed (changes tested before promotion to production)

databases: RDS Aurora (explicitly named in network diagram)
file_storage: S3 (S3 Document Store explicitly shown in network diagram)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (CC7.4.2: 'Backups are taken daily, multi-availability zones are configured')

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (for infrastructure accounts) + Clerk (web-app user authentication per network diagram)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; source code tool access requires MFA; email access requires 2FA
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS password policy: periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements, account lockout after limited failed attempts

encryption.at_rest: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: GitHub (inferred — branch protection, MFA for SCM, merge approval rules referenced)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not explicitly named (likely GitHub Actions given EKS/GitHub-native stack)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to move change to production environment (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations and rollback capability (specific tool not named — likely Terraform or Helm given EKS architecture)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned daily for patch compliance; OS patches applied automatically

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Datadog (explicitly listed as cloud subprocessor for 'logging and monitoring' in network diagram)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Datadog (confirmed subprocessor for logging); logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configurations, permissions, roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events; incident management process invoked for confirmed events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure — compute (EKS), networking (VPC, API Gateway), storage (S3, RDS Aurora), key management (KMS)
LLM inference — AI engine powering documentation generation and updates
Logging and monitoring — cloud subprocessor for observability
Source code triggers (PR events) and SCM for Promptless codebase
Trigger source (Slack DMs trigger documentation updates)
Trigger source (Zendesk tickets trigger documentation updates)

third_party_services.vendor: ReadMe / Mintlify / Intercom / Docusaurus / GitHub Pages
third_party_services.purpose: Documentation platform destinations — Promptless writes updated docs to these platforms
Project management integration for trigger events

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution installed on all desktops and laptops; anti-virus definitions updated daily; anti-virus on production servers with weekly definition updates and continuous scanning
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scans prior to every production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party; high/critical findings remediated and tracked (CC4.1.1 confirmed in test results)

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant changes to key personnel roles/responsibilities during engagement period
CC3.2.1 — Cybersecurity insurance control could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 — Customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 — Incident response policy effectiveness could not be tested because there were no security incidents during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center facilities: AWS is responsible for physical security; not in scope
A1.2 — Environmental protections: Promptless has no office; environmental controls managed by building management / AWS
Processing Integrity — Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy — Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC7.4.2: Daily backups confirmed with multi-AZ configuration and automated alerts
CC8.1.2: Branch protection enforced — merge to production requires additional approval
CC8.1.3: Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author; MFA required for SCM access
CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC6.2.2: Quarterly access reviews for all user accounts including privileged accounts
CC6.1.4: Access revoked within one business day of termination
CC7.1.2: Monthly automated vulnerability scans; remediation plan for critical/high findings
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meeting semiannually; IT Leadership Committee meeting weekly

infrastructure.rationale: Single region (us-east-1) with multi-AZ. EKS cluster is more sophisticated than simple ECS but still single-region. API Gateway as entry point. RDS Aurora is a notable choice (managed, scalable). No CDN or WAF. No multi-region deployment.

application_architecture.rationale: Kubernetes (EKS) orchestration is architecturally mature for a startup. API Gateway → EKS → RDS Aurora + S3 is a clean, scalable pattern. Branch protection and separate dev/test environment confirmed. Programming languages and frameworks not disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: RDS Aurora specifically named (strong choice — managed, auto-scaling, multi-AZ). S3 Document Store explicitly shown. Daily backups with multi-AZ confirmed. No caching layer mentioned. No data warehouse. Data classification and retention policies referenced.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, MFA on admin and SCM access, quarterly access reviews, 1-business-day access revocation, IDS/IPS via AWS, endpoint protection with daily AV updates, cybersecurity insurance confirmed. No WAF identified despite internet-facing API Gateway. No SIEM (Datadog handles monitoring but not SIEM functionality).

devops_maturity.rationale: EKS container orchestration is a strong signal. Branch protection and emergency change process confirmed. IaC referenced (unnamed tool, likely Terraform/Helm). Separate dev/test environments. CI/CD tool not explicitly named. Monthly patch management. Small team limits maturity signal.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing conducted. Daily backups confirmed (CC7.4.2). Multi-AZ failover via RDS Aurora. No multi-region failover. RTO/RPO not disclosed.

vendor_diversity.rationale: AWS as primary infrastructure provider with Anthropic (LLM), Datadog (monitoring), Clerk (auth), and GitHub (SCM) as named additional vendors. Four distinct non-AWS critical vendors is better than most companies in this cohort. However, the product itself is a deep integration platform with 10+ external systems, each representing an additional dependency.

overall.rationale: Promptless stands out for naming specific technologies: RDS Aurora, EKS, Anthropic, Datadog, and Clerk are all explicitly identified — a rare level of transparency. Zero exceptions across 58 controls in 3-month Type 2 audit. EKS is the most sophisticated compute choice in this cohort. Key risks: heavy reliance on Anthropic as a single LLM provider, single-region deployment, no WAF, undisclosed RTO/RPO, and the product's 10+ integration targets represent a large attack surface.

red_flags: Single-region deployment (us-east-1 only) — no multi-region failover capability
Anthropic as sole LLM provider — concentration risk on a single AI vendor for core product functionality
No WAF despite internet-facing API Gateway endpoints
Programming languages and framework stack not disclosed
10+ external integration targets (GitHub, Slack, Zendesk, etc.) each represent credential storage and API key management requirements
yellow_flags: Auditor (Accorp Partners) is a lower-tier/newer SOC 2 audit firm — not Big 4 or well-known mid-tier
3-month observation window is minimum for Type 2 — limited operational history tested
No SIEM despite Datadog monitoring (Datadog can serve SIEM-like functions but not explicitly configured as such)
No SSO for customer authentication (Clerk used but SSO not mentioned)
CI/CD tool not named — cannot assess deployment automation maturity
Write access to customer documentation platforms (ReadMe, Mintlify, etc.) creates significant blast radius if credentials are compromised
green_flags: Zero exceptions across all 58 controls tested in Type 2 audit
RDS Aurora specifically named — production-grade managed database with auto-scaling and multi-AZ built in
EKS (Kubernetes) cluster — most sophisticated compute orchestration in this cohort, indicating architectural maturity
Anthropic, Datadog, and Clerk explicitly named as subprocessors — unusually transparent vendor disclosure for a startup
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Daily backups with multi-AZ confirmed and tested
MFA enforced on admin production access, email, and source code management
Quarterly access reviews and 1-business-day access revocation SLA
BCDR plan tested annually with scenarios
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly for security operations review
key_observations: Promptless is an AI doc automation platform — it requires write access to customers' documentation systems (ReadMe, Mintlify, GitHub Pages, etc.), meaning a security breach could result in malicious content injection into customer-facing product documentation

Anthropic is listed as a cloud subprocessor, meaning customer content (ticket text, PR descriptions, Slack messages) passes through Anthropic's infrastructure for LLM processing — this has data residency and confidentiality implications
EKS (Kubernetes) is notably more mature than ECS/Lambda seen at other companies this size — suggests engineering team has Kubernetes experience, which is uncommon for a seed/early-stage startup
RDS Aurora is explicitly named in the network diagram — this is significant because Aurora Serverless can auto-scale to zero, which aligns well with a startup's variable workload patterns

The integration ecosystem (GitHub, Jira, Linear, Slack, Teams, Zendesk, ReadMe, Mintlify, Intercom, Docusaurus) means Promptless holds OAuth tokens and API credentials for a large number of customer systems — key management and rotation practices for these credentials are not described
Clerk is used for web-app user authentication (not AWS Cognito or a custom auth layer) — this is a common modern choice but adds a third-party dependency to the authentication critical path
Controls table reuses Accorp Partners boilerplate across multiple criteria — same pattern as other companies in this cohort (Judgment Labs, Lucid Spring)
